???? What You’ll Do

  • Strong English communication skills

About Codecontent
‍CodeContent is a UK-based technical content company founded by Mostafa Ibrahim, a former software engineer at GoCardless and Arm. We have published 300+ technical articles across 18 clients.
We produce tutorials and deep technical blog posts for leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed authentication companies, and major cloud infrastructure providers. Our team of 7 is made up of engineers who write, not writers who research. 
We have been operating for 3+ years.

‍About the Role
‍We are hiring a Software Engineer (Technical Content) to build and explain real software and AI systems for developer audiences. This is not a writing-only role. You will implement, run, and validate real code, then turn that work into long-form technical blogs and tutorials for our clients.
You will work across a wide range of topics: system design, LLMs, RAG pipelines, agent-based workflows, APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools. 
The output is written content, but the work is engineering.Your work will be published on the engineering blogs of leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed startups, and major cloud infrastructure providers, reaching tens of thousands of developers worldwide.

‍Responsibilities 
Write long-form technical blogs and tutorials based on provided briefsImplement, run, and validate real code as part of every article.
Research and clearly explain software engineering and AI workflows to developer audiences.

Cover topics including:
Software engineering fundamentals and system design
AI/ML concepts, LLMs, RAG systems, and agent-based workflows
APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools
Revise drafts based on editorial and technical feedback
Deliver consistently and on time

‍How We Work
‍This is a full-time position (40 hours/week) and your primary professional commitment. We are a small, focused team where every person carries significant ownership.
‍
‍Compensation
EGP 30,000 – EGP 40,000 per month (based on experience)
Direct mentorship from engineers with 300+ published pieces
Fully remote
Exposure to 10+ tech stacks annually
Salary reviews every 6 months based on performance

‍Why This Role
‍This is a role for engineers who enjoy explaining how things work. 
You will spend your days building real systems and writing about them for technical audiences. No fluff, no marketing spin. 
You will gain deep experience writing production-grade technical content.
requirements:

Experience working as a software engineer or in an equivalent technical role
Ability to explain complex technical concepts clearly to developer audiences
Comfort working with real-world tools, libraries, APIs, and infrastructure
Strong written communication: You can write clearly, concisely, and accurately

‍Nice to Have

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and their APIs
Kubernetes or distributed systems experience
Previous exposure to developer-focused SaaS products or technical documentation

This is an unparalleled opportunity to immerse yourself in real-world construction projects, shaping the landscape and contributing to significant infrastructure development. Responsibilities That Inspire:

  • Assist in Site Supervision: Work closely with senior engineers to oversee daily site operations, ensuring adherence to project plans and specifications.
  • Technical Execution: Participate in the implementation of civil engineering designs, providing on-site technical support and solutions.
  • Drawing & Documentation: Utilize AutoCAD Software and AutoCAD 3D to review, interpret, and update construction drawings, maintaining accurate project documentation.

  • Collaboration & Communication: Actively engage with project managers, contractors, and other stakeholders, fostering effective communication and teamwork.

You'll gain hands-on experience with industry-standard tools like AutoCAD Software and AutoCAD 3D, while developing crucial soft skills such as Site Supervision, Project Coordination, Technical Drawing, Problem-Solving, Communication, Teamwork, Time Management, and meticulous Attention to Detail.

Growth Opportunities:

As an Entry Level Civil Site Engineer, you will be mentored by experienced professionals, providing a solid foundation for rapid career progression. We are committed to your development, offering continuous learning opportunities, exposure to diverse projects, and a clear path to becoming a leader in the engineering field.

Team & Culture:

Join a collaborative, energetic, and supportive team where innovation is encouraged, and every voice is valued. We pride ourselves on a culture of excellence, mutual respect, and a shared passion for building outstanding projects. You'll be part of a team that celebrates successes and tackles challenges together.

Your contributions will directly influence the successful execution of vital construction projects across Giza, Egypt. You will play a key role in bringing architectural visions to life, ensuring structural integrity, and contributing to the development of modern infrastructure that benefits communities.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
salary: {'min': None, 'max': None, 'currency': None, 'period': None, 'additionalDetails': None, 'isPaid': True}
description:
The problem

Energy Market is the big brother to the stock market and it has similar trading mechanisms and similar forecasting problems and we are building the first AI energy trader that makes energy trading more accessible and profitable

Energy prices change every few minutes. Batteries can profit from that volatility, buying low, selling high, and getting paid to stabilize the grid, but only if the trading decisions are faster and smarter than any human can manage.

That’s what we’re building at Solship: an AI that trades energy autonomously, 24/7. Our models run millions of simulations per minute to decide whether a battery should charge, discharge, or hold. Every decision has direct profit and loss impact, and we’re already generating 2× revenue for pilot partners.
 

What you’ll own

• Forecasting models — design and train the models that predict energy prices, market signals across multiple markets

• Real-time dispatch optimization — build the optimizer that decides battery actions every few minutes, combining ML and stochastic methods under hard physical constraints

• Production ML pipeline — deploy, monitor, and improve models that execute live trades autonomously. Uptime and accuracy matter here as much as research quality.

• Performance infrastructure — build the evaluation layer that tracks model performance against live market outcomes and flags degradation before it costs money

• Product surface — contribute to the frontend (React/TypeScript) that makes forecasts, trades, and performance transparent to customers

 

What you’ll get

• You will be building An AI Trader nothing more cool than that — It is one of the most interesting applied ML problems out there

• Equity — you’re a founding engineer, not employee #47. You’ll have real ownership in what we’re building.

• Full technical ownership — you’ll define the ML architecture, choose the tools, and set the direction. No layers of approval.

• Flexible, async work — we care about output, not hours logged. Work from wherever you do your best thinking. (Honest note: You will work a lot)
requirements:
What we’re looking for

Must-haves

•      3-7 years Hands-on experience with ML-based optimization — MPC, reinforcement learning, MLIP, stochastic optimization, or similar constrained decision-making problems

•      Background in time-series forecasting and control systems

•      Very strong Mathematical and Modelling skills

•      Strong Python engineering skills and a track record of shipping ML models to production

•      Comfort operating with high autonomy in a small team where you’ll define the technical direction, not just follow it

 

Strong advantages

•      Experience with energy markets, electricity pricing, or grid operations

•      Full-stack ability (React / TypeScript) for contributing directly to the product

Data Modeling:

  • Ensure consistency, accuracy, and integrity of data models across the organization.
  • Continuously refine models to improve performance and adaptability to evolving business requirements.
  • Establish and enforce data modeling standards and governance.

 

Data Engineering and Pipeline Development:

  • Build, optimize, and maintain automated ETL/ELT pipelines to process large-scale data.
  • Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade existing ETL solutions.
  • Enhance data infrastructure to support efficient storage, retrieval, and transformation.
  • Implement automation to reduce manual processes and increase workflow efficiency.
  • Ensure data pipelines are robust, scalable, and support real-time and batch processing.

  • Research and integrate cutting-edge tools and platforms to enhance data management capabilities and streamline processes.

  • Develop and execute a technology roadmap to ensure the team is using the best tools available to meet both current and future data needs.

 

Optimization & Performance Management:

  • Define and enforce data governance policies, ensuring compliance with GDPR, and other regulations.
  • Monitor and optimize database performance, query execution, and storage efficiency.
  • Manage data quality, metadata, and lineage to ensure reliability and transparency.
  • Implement proactive monitoring and alerting for data infrastructure health.

Collaboration with Cross-Functional Teams:

title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer Backend · Frontend · DevOps · Cloud Infrastructure
salary: {'min': None, 'max': None, 'currency': None, 'period': None, 'additionalDetails': None, 'isPaid': True}
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

DimCarpi GmbH is a Munich-based lifestyle and fashion brand — registered under HRB 304755 — that delivers elegant, functional apparel and accessories to a global audience. As we scale our digital infrastructure, we are building a world-class engineering team in Egypt.

 

We are looking for a Senior Full-Stack Engineer who is genuinely strong across the entire stack: from crafting pixel-perfect, high-performance frontends to designing fault-tolerant backend services and deploying them on modern cloud infrastructure. This is a high-impact, high-ownership role. Your code will be running in production, at scale, from day one.

 

You should care deeply about system reliability, security, and developer experience — not just feature delivery. If you enjoy solving hard infrastructure and architecture problems as much as writing clean application code, this role is built for you.

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Backend Engineering

  • Design, build, and maintain production-grade RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Node.js frameworks (Fastify, Express.js, NestJS) or Go (Gin/Echo) or Python (FastAPI)
  • Architect and implement microservices and event-driven systems that are decoupled, observable, and resilient
  • Manage database design, migrations, indexing strategies, and query optimization across PostgreSQL and MongoDB
  • Implement robust authentication and authorization systems — OAuth 2.0, JWT, RBAC, session management
  • Write well-tested, well-documented backend code with a focus on maintainability and long-term scalability

Frontend Engineering

  • Build high-performance, accessible frontend applications using React, Next.js (App Router / Server Components), Angular, or Vue.js / Vite
  • Apply modern state management patterns (Zustand, Redux Toolkit, React Query / TanStack Query)
  • Ensure excellent Core Web Vitals: optimize for LCP, FID, CLS, and TTI across all pages
  • Implement responsive, accessible UI using Tailwind CSS or component libraries (MUI, Radix UI)
  • Collaborate closely with product and design to translate wireframes into production-ready interfaces

Infrastructure & DevOps

  • Containerize services using Docker and orchestrate workloads with Kubernetes (K8s)
  • Deploy and operate applications on cloud platforms — AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront, Lambda), Hetzner Cloud, or DigitalOcean / Vultr
  • Configure and optimize NGINX as a reverse proxy, load balancer, and TLS termination point
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar tools
  • Set up monitoring, alerting, and distributed tracing using Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry, or Datadog
  • Manage infrastructure as code using Terraform or Pulumi

Performance, Security & Reliability

  • Identify and eliminate performance bottlenecks across frontend (bundle size, render blocking, lazy loading) and backend (N+1 queries, slow endpoints, inefficient caching)
  • Implement multi-layer caching strategies: Redis (in-memory), CDN (CloudFront, Cloudflare), and HTTP cache headers
  • Apply security best practices: input validation and sanitization, protection against OWASP Top 10 (XSS, CSRF, SQL injection, IDOR), secrets management, and dependency auditing
  • Design for high availability: implement retries, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, health checks, and automated failover
  • Define and monitor SLOs and uptime targets; own incident response for services you build

 

TECH STACK & TOOLS

Backend Frameworks

Node.js: Fastify · Express.js · NestJS    |    Python: FastAPI    |   Go: Gin · Echo    |    .NET (C#): ASP.NET Core

Frontend Frameworks

React · Next.js (App Router) · Angular · Vue.js / Vite · TypeScript

Databases

PostgreSQL · MongoDB · Redis · Prisma ORM · TypeORM · Mongoose

Cloud & Infrastructure

AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront, Lambda, SQS) · Hetzner Cloud · DigitalOcean · Cloudflare

DevOps & Tooling

Docker · Kubernetes · NGINX · Terraform · GitHub Actions · GitLab CI · Prometheus · Grafana · Sentry · Datadog · ELK Stack

Other

GraphQL · REST · WebSockets · RabbitMQ / Kafka · Git · Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)

 

 

 

ABOUT DIMCARPI GMBH

DimCarpi GmbH is a Munich-based lifestyle and fashion brand committed to combining elegant design with practical functionality. We operate internationally, with a growing engineering team in Egypt driving the digital side of the business.

 

We believe great software is built by engineers who have context, ownership, and space to do their best work. We are building that kind of team.

 

Registered under HRB 304755, Munich, Germany.

 

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. We evaluate candidates on demonstrated ability and practical experience, not credentials alone.
requirements:
REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • 2 – 5 years of hands-on professional experience in full-stack software development
  • Deep working knowledge of at least one backend framework (Fastify, NestJS, FastAPI, Gin, or ASP.NET Core)
  • Solid frontend experience with React or Next.js — you understand how the browser renders and optimizes content, not just how to write components
  • Strong SQL fundamentals: schema design, normalization, indexing, query planning, and performance tuning in PostgreSQL
  • Experience with MongoDB: schema design for document-oriented data, aggregation pipelines, indexing strategies
  • Practical experience with Docker — containerizing applications, writing multi-stage Dockerfiles, running Docker Compose environments
  • Familiarity with at least one cloud platform — AWS, Hetzner, or equivalent — for deploying and operating live production services
  • Understanding of web performance: what affects load times, how to measure it (Lighthouse, WebPageTest, Core Web Vitals), and how to fix it
  • Solid grasp of web security principles: OWASP Top 10, secure coding practices, secrets management, and dependency vulnerability awareness
  • Experience writing unit, integration, and end-to-end tests (Jest, Vitest, Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent)
  • Strong command of English for asynchronous and synchronous communication with an international team

 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Experience with Kubernetes: deploying services, managing pods and namespaces, writing Helm charts
  • Hands-on experience with NGINX: configuring reverse proxies, rate limiting, SSL/TLS termination, and load balancing
  • Experience with Hetzner Cloud or similar European cloud providers in addition to or instead of AWS
  • Exposure to Go (.NET) or Python in a backend production context
  • Familiarity with message brokers (RabbitMQ, Kafka, or AWS SQS/SNS) for asynchronous processing
  • Experience in e-commerce, SaaS, or high-traffic consumer applications
  • Knowledge of observability practices: structured logging, distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), and metrics dashboards
  • Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent practical experience)

 

WHO YOU ARE

  • You take ownership — when you ship something, you care about what happens after it goes live
  • You communicate clearly and proactively, especially in a remote-first environment
  • You think in systems, not just features — you consider how your code affects reliability, observability, and other engineers
  • You are curious and self-directed: you do not wait to be told what to learn
  • You are comfortable with ambiguity and can scope and prioritize your own work
  • You give and receive feedback professionally and constructively
  • You are persistent — you debug hard problems methodically and see them through to resolution

 

WHAT WE OFFER

  • Competitive salary benchmarked against international engineering markets
  • Equity / profit-sharing opportunities as the team scales
  • Fully remote role with flexible working hours aligned to a Central European timezone
  • Direct access to senior leadership and meaningful technical influence over product and architecture decisions
  • A small, focused team where your contributions are visible and impactful
  • Work on a real, live product used by customers — not internal tooling
  • Opportunities to grow into technical lead or architect roles
  • Budget for learning resources, courses, and conferences

title: Senior AI Engineer
salary: {'min': None, 'max': None, 'currency': None, 'period': None, 'additionalDetails': None, 'isPaid': True}
description:
About the Role

We are seeking an engineer who doesn't just "talk" to LLMs but builds autonomous, resilient systems. You will design multi-agent architecture that can reason, use tools, and recover from failures independently.
 

Your primary goal is to bridge the gap between "cool demos" and "production-grade reliability." You will be responsible for deploying agents across various business sectors (e.g., Finance, Operations, Customer Success), ensuring they are not only intelligent but also safe, cost-effective, and predictable in a production environment.
 

Key Responsibilities
 

●  Agent Orchestration: Design and implement stateful, multi-turn agent workflows using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, PydanticAI, Swarm, Haystack, or Bee Agent Framework.
 

● Proactive Reliability & Guardrails: Architect systems that prevent production meltdowns. Implement circuit breakers, "human-in-the-loop" triggers, and input/output guardrails to stop infinite loops, prompt injections, and hallucinated tool calls before they reach the end user.
 

● Multi-Sector Tooling: Build and maintain high-precision API integrations (tools) that allow agents to interact with diverse business systems (ERPs, CRMs, custom databases) deterministically.
 

● Observability & Tracing: Set up advanced tracing (e.g., LangSmith, Langfuse, or Arize Phoenix) to debug complex reasoning chains and monitor agent trajectories in real-time.
 

● Rigorous Evaluation (Evals): Develop automated "Golden Datasets" and evaluation frameworks (using RAGas, DeepEval, G-Eval, LangSmith Evaluators, or custom model-based grading) to measure agent success rates and prevent regressions before every deployment.
 

● Cost & Latency Optimization: Manage the "token budget" by implementing tiered model routing (e.g., using Gemini 3 Flash for initial reasoning and Ultra for final verification) and optimizing context window usage.
requirements:
Required Qualifications
 

●  3+ years of Software Engineering experience, with a background in Python and FastAPI (or similar).
 

● 1+ year of experience specifically focused on LLM-based Agents in a production environment.
 

● Deep Expertise in Agentic Patterns: Proven experience implementing ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, or Multi-Agent Supervisor architectures.
 

● Production Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with modern Vector Databases and Search Engines (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant, Chroma, or pgvector) and semantic caching strategies.
 

● Robustness Mindset: A track record of handling non-deterministic failures (e.g., implementing custom retry logic and fallback strategies for failed tool calls).
 

Preferred Qualifications (The "Plus" Factors)
 

● Experience with Fine-tuning smaller models (e.g., Llama 3) for specific tool-calling tasks to reduce latency.
 

● Knowledge of Data Privacy (handling PII in agent prompts) and AI Security best practices.
 

● Prior experience building agents for specialized sectors like Fintech or Supply Chain.

Job Description:

As an R&D Product Development Engineer, you will contribute to a dynamic team responsible for the design, development, maintaining and launch of home appliance product ranges, including Electric Storage Water Heaters, Towel Dryers, and Domestic Water Heater Heat Pumps. Your role is to ensure that each new product meets key criteria for performance, safety, cost-efficiency, and environmental compliance, while staying aligned with both market demands and the company’s strategic goals.

Key Responsibilities:

 

New Product Design & Development

  • Design and develop new products, including integration of mechanical, thermal, and electrical subsystems.
  • Create 3D models, technical drawings, and product documentation using CAD software – PTC Creo
  • Evaluate new product concepts through prototyping and initial validation testing.
  • Collaborate with local and international cross-functional teams (Project Management, Marketing, Industrialization, Quality, Procurement, Supply Chain, Production etc.) from concept to launch.
  • Select appropriate materials and components to meet product requirements based on emerging trends and regulations (e.g., ERP, REACH, ROHS).
  • Prepare technical samples for laboratory testing, certification and marketing/customer needs for approval.

Engineering Changes:

You will be responsible for managing engineering change requests (ECRs) throughout the product lifecycle. This includes:

  • Co-ordination for launching new product variants on production lines.
  • Analyzing feedback from production, quality, and the field to identify improvement opportunities or solve potential market feedback.
  • Proposing, validating, and implementing design modifications to enhance product reliability, manufacturability, and cost-effectiveness.
  • Maintaining technical documentation (drawings, specifications, BOMs, work instructions) and ensuring traceability of changes through established change control processes (Engineering Change Management).
  • Coordinating with cross-functional teams to evaluate the impact of changes on production, product cost, suppliers, and regulatory compliance.
  • Manage phase out activities of obsolete ranges including stock management.
  • Participate in risk assessments, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA & PFMEA) for the proposed changes.

Industrialization Support

  • Support the transfer of designs to production, including BOM creation, work instructions, small product related tooling, etc...
  • Facilitate operator training for new line implementations.
  • Lead or support pilot production runs (Technical trials or Pre-Mass production runs).
  • Document trial runs by authoring trial reports and manage to get collective approval for production.
requirements:

Summary:
 

The Product Manager will be responsible for owning the product vision, strategy, and roadmap while ensuring successful execution through close collaboration with cross-functional teams. The role bridges business, technology, and customer needs, translating strategic objectives into prioritized product initiatives that deliver measurable value.

The ideal candidate has hands-on experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or Business Analyst and is comfortable operating across discovery, delivery, and optimization phases. They will work closely with stakeholders, customers, and development teams to define product direction, prioritize initiatives, and ensure timely, high-quality delivery aligned with business goals.
 

Roles & Responsibilities:

Product Strategy & Vision

  • Define and communicate a clear product vision aligned with business strategy and customer needs.
  • Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with organizational priorities and market demands.
  • Continuously evaluate product performance and identify opportunities for improvement, optimization, or innovation.
  • Balance short-term delivery needs with long-term product strategy.
     

Stakeholder & Customer Management

  • Act as the primary point of contact between business stakeholders, customers, and delivery teams.
  • Gather, analyze, and validate requirements from business users and stakeholders.
  • Understand and prioritize end-user needs, preferences, and pain points.
  • Ensure stakeholder expectations are aligned with product scope, timelines, and outcomes.
  • Conduct internal product review sessions to assess progress and manage expectations.
     

Backlog & Requirements Management

  • Own and continuously refine the product backlog to maximize business value.
  • Translate business and customer needs into clear, concise, and actionable user stories.
  • Break down complex requirements into manageable features and user stories.
  • Define, document, and validate acceptance criteria for user stories and features.
  • Establish and maintain business acceptance criteria in collaboration with stakeholders.
  • Prioritize backlog items based on business value, risk, dependencies, and strategic goals.
     

Agile Delivery & Execution

  • Work closely with Scrum/Kanban teams to ensure effective planning, execution, and delivery.
  • Participate in sprint planning, backlog grooming, reviews, and retrospectives.
  • Evaluate product progress at each iteration and provide insights for continuous improvement.
  • Provide timely and clear feedback to development teams to ensure alignment with product objectives.
  • Validate the Definition of Done for user stories, iterations, and releases.
  • Support release planning and oversee iteration and release schedules.
     

Metrics, Reporting & Continuous Improvement

  • Monitor delivery performance using metrics such as velocity, burndown/burnup, and throughput.
  • Track iteration progress and ensure transparency across teams and stakeholders.
  • Ensure customer feedback is captured, categorized, and translated into actionable backlog items.
  • Promote Agile best practices and a culture of continuous improvement and learning.
  • Support teams in improving processes, quality, and delivery efficiency.
requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (preferred).
  • 3 to 6 years of experience as a Product Manager, Product Owner, or Business Analyst.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies, particularly Scrum and Kanban.
  • Proven experience with user stories, acceptance criteria, and backlog management.
  • Familiarity with modern software development practices, including CI/CD, automated testing, and TDD concepts.
  • Solid understanding of software development lifecycles and service-oriented architectures.
  • Experience using metrics to drive product decisions and incremental delivery.
  • Knowledge of backlog tracking, velocity, burndown/burnup charts, and capacity planning.
  • Strong communication, stakeholder management, and decision-making skills.
  • Ability to take ownership, manage priorities, and deliver products within agreed timelines.

description:

Join our dynamic team in Giza, Egypt as a Project Manager (Civil Engineer) and take the helm of impactful construction projects. This is an unparalleled opportunity to leverage your expertise, drive innovation, and leave a lasting mark on the landscape of Egypt!

Overview: We are seeking an exceptional Project Manager with a robust background in Civil Engineering to lead complex construction projects from inception to successful completion. Growth Opportunities: We are committed to your professional development. As a Project Manager, you will have access to continuous learning, mentorship, and opportunities to lead increasingly complex and high-profile projects, paving the way for senior leadership roles within our organization.

Team & Culture: Join a vibrant and supportive team that values innovation, collaboration, and excellence. We foster a culture of respect, accountability, and continuous improvement, where every team member is empowered to contribute to our collective success. You will be part of an on-site team deeply integrated into the local community and infrastructure development.

Impact: Your work will directly shape the infrastructure of Giza and beyond. You will be responsible for delivering projects that enhance communities, support economic growth, and contribute to the national development goals, making a tangible and significant impact.

description:
Job Title: Generative AI Engineer (GenAI Engineer)

Location: Remote / Hybrid (Flexible) Department: AI/ML Engineering Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description

We are looking for a talented Generative AI Engineer to design, build, and optimize production-grade generative AI applications. You will work on end-to-end AI solutions that combine large language models (LLMs), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agentic workflows, and multimodal capabilities to deliver intelligent, scalable, and reliable AI products.

You will be a key member of a high-performing team building next-generation AI systems using modern frameworks and tools.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and maintain production-ready GenAI applications using LangChain, LangGraph, and related orchestration frameworks.
  • Build advanced RAG pipelines (including multi-stage retrieval, reranking, query transformation, and evaluation).
  • Implement and optimize agentic workflows with LangGraph (stateful multi-agent systems, tool-calling, memory, human-in-the-loop).
  • Work with vector databases (primarily Qdrant, also Pinecone, Weaviate, or Chroma) for efficient semantic search and retrieval.
  • Fine-tune, quantize, and deploy LLMs and embedding models using PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, and PEFT methods (LoRA, QLoRA).
  • Integrate and evaluate open-source and proprietary models (Llama 3, Mistral, Mixtral, GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.).
  • Implement evaluation frameworks (RAGAS, ARES, DeepEval, LangSmith) and monitoring for LLM applications.
  • Optimize for cost, latency, and accuracy in production environments.
  • Collaborate with backend, frontend, and data teams to integrate AI capabilities into user-facing products.
  • Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in LLMOps, agentic AI, and generative technologies.

Technical Requirements (Must-Have)

  • Strong proficiency in Python (advanced scripting, OOP, async programming, typing).
  • Deep experience with LangChain & LangGraph (at least 1+ year of hands-on production use).
  • Solid RAG expertise: advanced retrieval strategies, chunking, metadata filtering, hybrid search, reranking, and evaluation.
  • Hands-on experience with Qdrant (or equivalent vector DBs) — collection management, filtering, hybrid search, payload optimization.
  • PyTorch experience: model fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, inference optimization (bitsandbytes, vLLM, TorchServe), custom training loops.
  • Experience deploying LLMs (vLLM, TGI, Ollama, or cloud services like AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI).
  • Strong understanding of LLMOps (LangSmith, Phoenix, Helicone, Prometheus + Grafana).
  • Experience with modern MLOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, GitHub Actions).
requirements:
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or equivalent experience.
  • 3+ years of hands-on experience building production GenAI / LLM applications.
  • Proven track record of deploying scalable RAG and agent systems.
  • Strong software engineering fundamentals (clean code, testing, design patterns, API development — FastAPI preferred).
  • Published projects, GitHub repositories, or contributions to open-source LangChain/LangGraph ecosystem.

title: Senior Electrical & Controls Engineer
salary: {'min': None, 'max': None, 'currency': None, 'period': None, 'additionalDetails': None, 'isPaid': True}
description:
Job Purpose:

The Senior Electrical & Controls Engineer leads and supports the electrical, controls, automation, and commissioning scope for bulk material handling systems and machine packages. The role covers the project lifecycle from concept and design review through technical coordination, testing, commissioning, startup, troubleshooting, and handover. The position plays a key technical role in ensuring that machine functionality, control philosophy, and field performance are aligned with project and client requirements.
requirements:
Key Responsibilities

Electrical and Controls Engineering

  • Lead and support the electrical and controls engineering scope for bulk material handling systems such as conveyors, feeders, transfer systems, bunker discharge systems, silo discharge systems, and related auxiliary equipment.
  • Prepare, review, and validate key engineering deliverables including single line diagrams, control schematics, panel layouts, cable schedules, I/O lists, loop diagrams, motor lists, and commissioning documentation.
  • Define and review control philosophy, interlocks, permissives, alarms, trips, operating sequences, and field device interfaces.

Design Coordination and Technical Leadership

  • Act as the senior focal point for electrical and controls matters within assigned projects or machine packages.
  • Coordinate with mechanical, process, project, and procurement teams to ensure full alignment of electrical and controls scope with machine function and operating requirements.
  • Review vendor submittals, technical offers, datasheets, and design documents to confirm compliance with project specifications and client standards.
  • Participate in technical meetings, design reviews, risk reviews, and client discussions.
  • Provide guidance and technical support to junior engineers and project team members.

Commissioning and Startup

  • Lead or support pre-commissioning, cold commissioning, hot commissioning, startup, functional testing, and performance verification activities.
  • Review electrical and controls readiness before energization and startup.
  • Support loop testing, signal verification, motor rotation checks, functional checks, sequence testing, and interlock validation.
  • Troubleshoot electrical and automation issues during startup and propose practical corrective actions.
  • Coordinate with project teams, vendors, site teams, and client representatives during testing and commissioning phases.
  • Prepare commissioning reports, punch lists, startup records, and handover documentation.

Site and Project Support

  • Provide technical support during project execution to ensure that the electrical and controls scope is implemented in line with design intent and operating philosophy.
  • Resolve technical queries, review site feedback, and support practical engineering decisions during execution.
  • Attend site visits as required for inspections, testing, commissioning, startup, troubleshooting, and technical coordination.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering, Electrical Power, Automation, Mechatronics, or a related discipline.
  • Strong experience in electrical, EC&I, controls, or commissioning roles within industrial projects.
  • Proven exposure to heavy industry, cement, mining, metals, alternative fuels, waste handling, or bulk material handling environments.
  • Strong knowledge of MCCs, PLCs, drives, control panels, instrumentation, and industrial electrical systems.
  • Strong ability to read and review electrical drawings, control schematics, P&IDs, logic documents, and interface drawings.
  • Good command of English for technical communication, reporting, and coordination.

Preferred Experience

  • Experience with bulk material handling systems such as conveyors, feeders, silos, bunkers, transfer points, and discharge systems.
  • Familiarity with Siemens, Schneider, Allen-Bradley, Yokogawa, or similar automation platforms.
  • Experience using AutoCAD, EPLAN, ETAP, Ecodial, or similar engineering software.
  • Experience in FAT, SAT, commissioning planning, and project handover.
  • Experience in OEM, EPC, turnkey, or machine-package project environments.

Seniority Expectations

The Senior Electrical & Controls Engineer is expected to take ownership of the electrical and controls scope for assigned projects or packages, make sound technical decisions, represent the function in technical meetings, support successful commissioning and startup, and contribute to the overall technical success of the project.

Typical Deliverables

  • Reviewed and approved electrical drawings
  • Control and interlock philosophy input
  • I/O and loop documentation
  • Vendor review comments and technical clarifications
  • Commissioning checklists and procedures
  • Startup and troubleshooting reports
  • Punch lists and closeout records
  • As-built review comments

description:
Key Responsibilities

Security Strategy & Governance
Develop, implement, and maintain the organization's information security strategy, policies, and standards aligned with business objectives and regulatory requirements (ISO 27001, NIST, HIPA).


Risk Management
Identify, assess, and priorities information security risks. Maintain the risk register and drive remediation plans in collaboration with stakeholders across all business units.
Security Operations Oversight
Oversee day-to-day SOC operations including SIEM monitoring, incident triage, threat intelligence, and EDR management to ensure timely detection and response to threats.
Incident Response & Crisis Management
Lead the response to security incidents, breaches, and crises. Own the Incident Response Plan, conduct post-incident reviews, and drive continuous improvement of detection and response capabilities.
Compliance & Audit
Ensure adherence to applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations. Manage internal and external audits, track findings to closure, and maintain compliance evidence documentation.
Vulnerability & Penetration Testing
Define and oversee the vulnerability management lifecycle. Commission and review penetration testing engagements, priorities remediation, and report on security posture trends to executive leadership.

Security Awareness & Culture
Design and deliver security awareness training programs. 

 Champion a security-first culture across the organization and communicate risk clearly to non-technical audiences.
Team Leadership & Development
Lead, mentor, and develop the security team. Define KPI frameworks, conduct performance reviews, manage workloads, and build talent pipelines to support organizational growth.
Security Architecture & Projects
Provide security guidance on new projects, system designs, and technology adoptions. Collaborate with IT and engineering teams to embed security by design into all initiatives.
Reporting & Executive Communication
Produce regular security dashboards, board-level risk reports, and KPI metrics. Translate complex technical risk into clear business impact narratives for senior leadership.

description:
About the Role

We are a US-based, successful e-commerce and EdTech company. We are looking for a sharp, AI-native graduate who thinks in systems and solves problems with automation. This is not a traditional software engineering role — you won't spend your days writing code. Instead, you will use cutting-edge AI tools, particularly Claude Code, to design, build, and manage intelligent systems that automate our core business processes across technology, marketing, and operations.

 

This is a rare opportunity to join at the forefront of an AI-driven transformation. If you are someone who naturally asks 'how can AI do this better?' — this role was built for you.

Key Responsibilities

AI Systems & Automation

  • Design and deploy end-to-end automation workflows using Claude Code and other AI tools to eliminate manual, repetitive business processes.
  • Build and manage AI-powered systems that integrate across our tech stack, including our membership platform, CRM, and marketing tools.
  • Continuously identify automation opportunities across all departments and proactively propose solutions.
  • Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimise AI-generated outputs to ensure reliability and accuracy.

PHP Membership Platform Support

  • Work alongside our development infrastructure to support and enhance our PHP-based membership system using Claude Code.
  • Understand and interpret PHP code well enough to identify issues, request AI-generated fixes, and validate solutions without necessarily coding from scratch.
  • Coordinate with external developers when deeper technical intervention is required.

Marketing Automation

  • Use AI tools to automate marketing workflows including email campaigns, lead nurturing, content scheduling, and funnel management.
  • Build and maintain integrations between marketing platforms (e.g. ActiveCampaign, Mailchimp) and our core systems using AI and automation tools like Make or Zapier.
  • Generate, optimise, and deploy AI-assisted marketing content at scale.

Systems Thinking & Continuous Improvement

  • Map out existing business processes and redesign them with an automation-first mindset.
  • Document all systems, workflows, and automations clearly for team handover and scalability.
  • Stay up to date with the latest developments in AI tooling and bring new ideas to the team regularly.

Essential

  • Demonstrated ability to build automated workflows and systems using AI — portfolio or project examples strongly preferred.
  • Technical literacy: ability to read, understand, and troubleshoot PHP, HTML, and basic code.
  • Make (Integromat), Zapier, or n8nFamiliarity with integration platforms such as
  • Understanding of APIs, webhooks, and how digital systems connect.
  • Exceptional problem-solving ability with a systems-thinking mindset.
  • Self-driven, proactive, and comfortable working independently with minimal supervision.

Preferred

  • Experience with marketing automation platforms (ActiveCampaign, Mailchimp, HubSpot, etc.)
  • Familiarity with CRM systems and sales funnel management.
  • Basic understanding of prompt engineering and LLM capabilities.
  • Prior internship, freelance project, or personal AI automation project — even if not in a formal role.

Education

  • Bachelor's degree from a top Egyptian university (e.g. AUC, Cairo University, Ain Shams, GUC) in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Technology, or a related field.
  • Graduation year: 2023–2025 preferred.

What We Offer

  • A highly dynamic, forward-thinking environment where AI is at the centre of everything we do.
  • The opportunity to own and build systems from the ground up — not just maintain them.
  • 10,000 – 15,000 EGP/monthA competitive graduate salary of
  • Continuous learning and exposure to the latest AI tools, platforms, and methodologies.
  • Clear growth path into a senior AI systems or operations leadership role.
  • A collaborative team that values initiative, creativity, and results over rigid hierarchy.

The Ideal Candidate

More than your degree or your technical background, we care about your mindset. The ideal candidate:

 

  • Thinks in systems — you don't just solve problems, you build processes that prevent them.
  • Is AI-native — you already use Claude, ChatGPT, or similar tools daily and have built something with them.
  • Is relentlessly curious — you follow AI developments closely and get excited about what's possible.
  • Takes ownership — when something needs to be done, you figure out how and get it done.
  • Communicates clearly — you can explain a technical system to a non-technical stakeholder without jargon.

description:
Are you an ambitious engineer ready to ignite your career in the heart of Cairo? We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Technical Office Engineer to join our dynamic, full-time, on-site team! This is an unparalleled opportunity for an entry-level professional with 2-5 years of experience to make a significant impact in complex engineering projects, focusing on cutting-edge Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems, including advanced Firefighting and HVAC solutions.

Overview: Join a pioneering engineering firm renowned for delivering excellence across diverse projects. As a Technical Office Engineer, you will be instrumental in the design, coordination, and execution phases, ensuring precision and compliance with the highest industry standards. Based in our bustling Cairo office, you will be at the forefront of innovation, transforming blueprints into tangible realities.

  • Develop and review detailed technical drawings, specifications, and reports for MEP systems, with a strong emphasis on Firefighting and HVAC designs.
  • Utilize expertly software such as AutoCAD and Autodesk Revit to produce accurate and efficient project documentation.
  • Prepare and manage Bill of Quantities (BOQ) and assist in cost estimation, contributing to successful project budgeting and tendering processes.
  • Coordinate closely with design teams, site engineers, and clients to ensure seamless project execution and resolve technical challenges proactively.
  • Contribute to the optimization of engineering solutions, ensuring compliance with local and international codes and standards.
  • Support the tendering and bidding process by preparing comprehensive technical submissions.

Skills & Technologies: You will be immersed in a world of advanced engineering. We expect proficiency in MEP Engineering, specializing in Firefighting System Design and HVAC System Design. Mastery of AutoCAD and Autodesk Revit is essential, alongside strong skills in Technical Drawing, Engineering Calculations, and Project Coordination.

Growth Opportunities: This entry-level role is a springboard for incredible career advancement. You'll gain hands-on experience with diverse projects, work alongside industry veterans, and have opportunities for continuous professional development, mentorship, and specializations within MEP, project management, or design leadership.

Team & Culture: We foster a collaborative, supportive, and high-performance culture. Our Cairo office is a hub of innovation where ideas are shared freely, and every team member's contribution is valued. We believe in empowering our engineers to take ownership and grow their expertise in a positive and engaging environment.

Impact: Your work will directly shape the built environment of Cairo and beyond. By ensuring the integrity and efficiency of essential building systems, you will contribute to sustainable infrastructure, safety, and comfort for countless individuals. Your precision and expertise will be critical to the successful delivery of landmark projects.
requirements:
• Required Skills: Possess a robust skill set including Firefighting System Design, HVAC System Design, and comprehensive knowledge of MEP Engineering. Demonstrate expert proficiency in AutoCAD and Autodesk Revit. Strong capabilities in Technical Drawing, Project Coordination, Bill of Quantities (BOQ) Preparation, Cost Estimation, Engineering Calculations, Site Coordination, Tendering & Bidding Support, Communication Skills, and Problem Solving are essential.
  • Educational Background: A Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related engineering discipline from an accredited university.
  • Experience: An impressive 2 to 5 years of professional experience in an engineering or technical office role, preferably within the MEP sector.
  • Career Level: This is an exceptional opportunity for an Entry Level professional eager to launch and accelerate their career with significant responsibilities and growth potential.

title: Senior HSE Engineer
salary: {'min': None, 'max': None, 'currency': None, 'period': None, 'additionalDetails': None, 'isPaid': True}
description:
1) Compliance & Governance

  • Ensure adherence to Egyptian laws and regulations (e.g., environmental permits, hazardous substances, civil defense/fire codes, labor law) and local authority/site licensing requirements.
  • Maintain and improve ISO 45001 (OHSMS) and ISO 14001 (EMS) management systems; lead internal audits, corrective actions, and surveillance/recertification preparations.
  • Develop, review, and roll out HSE policies, procedures, SOPs, and permit‑to‑work systems across all retail sites.

2) Risk Management & Process Safety (Retail Focus)

  • Conduct and update risk assessments (HIRA/JSA), bow‑ties, and job safety analyses for fueling, tanker offloading, tank gauging, confined space entry, hot works, and working at height.
  • Lead process safety controls for storage tanks, piping, dispensers, vapor recovery, pressure relief, and electrical bonding/earthing; verify integrity through inspections and testing.
  • Implement and monitor contractor HSE requirements (pre‑qualification, onboarding, method statements, tool‑box talks, supervision).

3) Site Assurance & Inspections

  • Plan and perform station audits/inspections (housekeeping, ATEX/EX equipment, emergency equipment, signage, spill kits, firefighting systems, separators).
  • Track actions to closure; report trends and recommend engineering and behavioral controls.
  • Verify tanker fleet compliance (ADR-equivalent practices, driver competence, defensive driving, spill prevention).

4) Incident Prevention, Reporting & Learning

  • Lead incident/near‑miss investigations using root cause techniques (5‑Why, ICAM, TapRooT® style), issue reports, and drive corrective/preventive actions.
  • Champion HSE reporting pyramid/iceberg concepts to boost near‑miss, hazard observation, and unsafe condition reporting and learning (REX).
  • Manage statutory emergency response arrangements, drills, and coordination with local authorities and Civil Defense.

5) Training, Culture & Engagement

  • Deliver and track HSE training (induction, PTW, LOTO, confined space, working at height, spill response, fire warden, manual handling).
  • Coach site managers/dealers and supervisors on behavior-based safety, leadership walkdowns, and toolbox talks.
  • Lead campaigns (heat stress, driving safety, slips/trips/falls) and Safety Days.

6) Environment & Sustainability

  • Oversee spill prevention and response, waste management, water/oil separator maintenance, and air emissions (vapor recovery).
  • Monitor utilities, fuel losses, and product variance; support leak detection, tank tightness testing, and remediation.
  • Track environmental KPIs and statutory reporting; support sustainability initiatives (energy efficiency, solar, EV/CNG expansion).

7) Data, Reporting & Continuous Improvement

  • Maintain HSE dashboards (LTIFR, TRIR, near‑miss rate, audit closure rate, training completion).
  • Present monthly HSE reports to management; recommend risk‑based investments and CAPEX for safety improvements.
  • Digitize HSE workflows (inspection checklists, incident reporting, action tracking) where possible.
requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, Electrical, Petroleum, or HSE).
  • 5–7+ years of progressive HSE experience, with at least 3–5 years in petroleum retail/fuel depots/CNG/LPG or equivalent high‑risk operations.
  • Certifications: NEBOSH IGC (minimum); ISO 45001 Lead Auditor and/or ISO 14001 Lead Auditor preferred; OSHA/IOSH an advantage.
  • Demonstrated experience with incident investigations, risk assessments, contractor HSE, and emergency response.
  • Strong knowledge of Egyptian regulatory requirements and international best practices (NFPA/API/IECEx/ATEX concepts applicable to retail).
  • Fluent English; strong report writing and presentation skills.
  • Willingness to travel nationwide (40–60%).

description:
Are you passionate about building the future of AI? We are seeking a talented AI Engineer to join our innovative team. You will play a key role in optimizing and extending our AI capabilities across Computer Vision, Speech Recognition, NLP (LLMs), and Generative AI. If you thrive on deploying models in real-world production environments and driving impactful AI solutions, we want to meet you!

 

You’ll work on enhancing our current models (YOLO, Riva) and developing new AI-powered features that fuel our products and services.

 

Key Responsibilities

  • Design & Develop: Build advanced machine learning and deep learning models for real-world applications.
  • Data Exploration: Collect, analyze, and interpret large-scale datasets to extract actionable insights and identify trends.
  • Model Optimization: Evaluate, benchmark, and optimize models for performance, scalability, and robustness.
  • Production Deployment: Lead the deployment of AI models into production using modern frameworks and tools.
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Work closely with product, engineering, and business teams to integrate AI solutions seamlessly.
  • Domain-Specific AI: Work with large construction datasets for predictive analytics and automation.
  • LLM Engineering: Design sophisticated prompts and fine-tune large language models for optimal results.
  • Data Visualization: Present insights using dashboards and visualization tools to stakeholders.
  • Documentation: Test, document, and communicate model performance, efficiency, and scalability.
  • Continuous Learning: Stay at the forefront of AI advancements, including multimodal and generative AI.
requirements:
What We’re Looking For

  • Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or related field.
  • Experience: 3–5 years of hands-on experience developing and deploying machine learning models in production.
  • Technical Skills:
  • Strong programming skills in Python; familiarity with data science libraries (NumPy, Pandas, etc.).
  • Deep expertise in ML/DL frameworks: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn.
  • Experience with Computer Vision (YOLO, OpenCV) and Speech Recognition (NVIDIA Riva, Whisper, Kaldi, etc.).
  • Knowledge of LLMs and prompt engineering; experience with generative AI is a plus.
  • Proficiency in model training, fine-tuning, and deployment (ONNX, TensorRT, etc.).
  • Familiarity with NVIDIA tools (CUDA Toolkit, TAO Toolkit, DeepStream) is a bonus.
  • Strong Linux skills (Ubuntu, Bash scripting, system configuration).
  • Soft Skills: Excellent communication, problem-solving, and teamwork abilities. Able to explain complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Bonus Points:
  • Experience with cloud AI platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, Azure ML).
  • MLOps experience (CI/CD, model monitoring, automated pipelines).

 

Why Join Us?

  • Innovative Environment: Work on cutting-edge AI solutions with a passionate, collaborative team.
  • Impact: Drive real-world AI applications in computer vision, speech, and NLP.
  • Growth: Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.
  • Flexibility: Supportive, flexible working environment.

  • I'm looking for one exceptional person. Not a task-follower. Not someone who waits to be told what to do. I'm looking for a true right-hand — someone who thinks ahead, takes ownership, and turns my intentions into reality.
  • If you've ever been told you're "too much" for a VA role — that you care too deeply, overdeliver when no one asked, or ask too many questions — keep reading. This might be your thing.
  • About Me
  • My name is Nephi. I'm an entrepreneur, real estate investor, and software founder based in Utah — for now. My partner Fernanda is from Brazil, and we're building toward living abroad, probably Latin America.
  • I work full-time as a Scrum Master — about 10-hour days. That's my primary income. My career went from mechanical engineering to manufacturing project management to where I am now, which shaped how I think: analytical, process-oriented, obsessed with continuous improvement. I'm a big fan of visual Kanban boards and dashboards — they create undeniable clarity in real time. The businesses are real and growing, but they haven't replaced my salary yet. I'm upfront about that because transparency is one of my core values.
  • I taught myself Spanish and Portuguese. I'm a competitive powerlifter (recently broke 1,000 lbs total). I completed 75 Hard. I'm a member of GoBundance, an entrepreneur mastermind where I facilitate a local pod. I've traveled extensively — Africa, Japan, the Philippines, Italy, Taiwan, Costa Rica. I'm open-minded, growth-oriented, and always consuming books and podcasts.
  • I have ADHD. I move fast, I have a million ideas, and I struggle with implementation — not because the ideas aren't good, but because executive dysfunction is real and my time is limited. The vision is there. What I need is someone who can take it and run.
  • What drives me: impact, connection, and variety. What I value most: kindness, transparency, speed, and playfulness. I like to laugh — a lot. If you can make me laugh in your application or on our first call, that'll go a long way. The agile mindset from my Scrum Master work carries into everything — constant iteration, transparency, inspection, and adaptation. Nothing is ever "done" — it's always getting better. I'm looking for someone who feels less like an employee and more like a partner. Someone who cares personally and challenges directly.
  • What I've Got Going On
  • Porch Light (tech startup) — this is the one I care about most. Mission-driven, addressing the loneliness epidemic. We have an MVP, no revenue yet, and I believe this has more potential to make a meaningful difference than anything else I'm working on.
  • A land flipping business (paused) — proven model. Paused due to costs and time. Could be revived with the right support.
  • Airbnb properties — operationally heavy for what they return. I need strategic thinking, not just task management.
  • Long-term rentals — a fourplex and other properties.
  • Personal admin — a constant backlog of tasks that never get done because I work 10-hour days.
  • Other ideas waiting for bandwidth — a podcast called Insight in Inmates, accessible Montessori-style education, and more.
  • There's a detailed briefing section at the bottom of this listing with full context on each business, my challenges, and my daily schedule. That's what you'll use for your 90-day plan.
  • What You'd Actually Do
  • I'll give you everything you need to succeed — my vision, my intentions, and what "good" looks like. How you get there is up to you.
  • Sometimes I'll call with something specific — "research apartments in Uruguay" or "schedule a Mexican passport appointment." But more often, I'll share where I want things to go, and you'll figure out the path. You know the Airbnb numbers need to improve, so you work through Karla (my co-host, who's great) to find solutions. You know Porch Light needs users, so you draft a growth strategy. You see a vendor isn't delivering, so you find a better one. You notice my calendar is a mess, so you fix it. Creativity and problem-solving — not just following instructions.
  • You'll manage my calendar, triage email, and handle communications on OpenPhone and WhatsApp — often as my voice, which means you need to understand how I think. I use OpenClaw (an AI assistant on WhatsApp) and I expect you to learn it and actively find ways AI tools can improve our operations.
  • After onboarding, I will not touch a keyboard for business operations. Everything goes through you. We'll connect by phone — twice a day at first, lunch and evening.
  • What's In It for You
  • You'll be paid well. Compensation reflects the caliber of person I'm looking for. Happy to discuss specifics with the right candidate.
  • You'll get mentorship and training most EAs never get access to. You'll be plugged into GoBundance's Second-in-Command program — dedicated training alongside the right-hand people of successful entrepreneurs and millionaires. These are people running operations for 7- and 8-figure businesses, and you'll be learning right alongside them. This isn't a webinar you watch alone — it's a real community that will push you to grow. On top of that, you'll have direct access to me: a certified Scrum Master, competitive powerlifter, multilingual world traveler, and someone who's invested tens of thousands of dollars in personal development programs from Tony Robbins to GoBundance to land investing masterminds. I share everything I learn.
  • You'll have real responsibility from day one. Deep access to my systems, real authority to make decisions, and increasing autonomy as we build trust. You won't be someone's inbox manager — as we grow together, you'll essentially be operating as my COO. Your work will directly impact the bottom line, and you'll see the results of your decisions in real time.
  • No two days will look the same. Real estate, a tech startup addressing the loneliness epidemic, land investing, international relocation planning, personal admin, vendor management, and strategy. If you've ever been bored at a job, this is the opposite of that.
  • You'll be part of something going somewhere. I'm relocating internationally. Porch Light is mission-driven and scalable. The businesses are evolving. You're not filling a seat that someone left — you're building a role that didn't exist before, alongside someone who will genuinely invest in your growth as a person, not just as an employee.

    Who You Are
  • I care far less about your experience and far more about how your brain works.
  • I'm not trying to hire someone less capable than me — I'm trying to do the opposite. I want someone sharper, more organized, and more detail-oriented than I am. I want to pay you well — genuinely above what your skills would typically command locally — because I'm not hiring for a task list. I'm hiring for a brain.
  • You act first and report back. My biggest pet peeve is "what should I do?" at every step. I'd rather you decide and be wrong than wait and slow things down. There's a story I love: three people are asked to buy oranges for someone's wife. The first just grabs oranges. The second asks the clerk for the best quality and price. The third calls the wife directly, finds out her favorite variety, drives to a better store, and brings them back with a note. I want the third person. They didn't need step-by-step instructions — they understood the intention and figured out the best way to get there. That's what working with me should feel like.
  • You're radically candid. Tell me when I'm wrong. Push back from kindness. Then execute once we've decided.
  • You're tech-savvy and AI-literate. I built my startup with Claude. Non-negotiable.
  • You're a personal development enthusiast. You'd have book and podcast recommendations for me, not just the other way around.
  • You thrive in ambiguity. Shifting priorities, a dozen contexts in one week, no clear-cut instructions. If that excites you, keep reading.
  • How to Apply
  • 1. Resume — informal is fine. I care about what you did, not your titles.
  • 2. Voice note (2–3 min max) — What stood out to you from this listing? Who are you as a person? What are you proud of outside of work? I want to hear your energy, not read a polished paragraph.
  • 3. DISC profile — take a free online assessment. I'm a high D / high C. Tell me why we'd click.
  • 4. Quick personal questions (I'll go first):
  • I'm the oldest sibling. What's your birth order?
  • My love languages are words of affirmation and quality time. What's yours?
  • Tony Robbins' Six Human Needs — my top two are contribution and variety. What are yours, and why?
  • What book, podcast, or idea changed how you think?
  • 5. Your 90-day plan — the most important part. Read the Briefing below. Then tell me: how would you start, what access do you need, what value do you deliver out of the gate, and what would you advise me to do strategically? Every recommendation should serve two goals: reducing my cognitive load and improving the bottom line.
requirements:
The Basics

  • Full-time, 40 hours/week
  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • Reliable internet, quiet workspace
  • Comfortable with technology and AI tools

 

  • The Briefing
  • My Daily Reality
  • Wake at 7. W-2 job 8–5. Gym 5:30–6:30. Time with Fernanda 7–10. I have 1–3 hours a day for my businesses but prefer to use evenings for personal time. Our calls happen while I'm walking — lunch and evening. After onboarding, everything flows through you.
  • Porch Light (Tech Startup) — My Top Priority
  • Remember when connecting with a friend was as simple as seeing their porch light on? Porch Light signals when you're available and lets you connect within minutes — reducing friction around quality conversations. An 8–10 minute call can significantly reduce loneliness. Built with Claude (AI) on React Native. We have an MVP but zero active users. I've been stuck perfecting instead of marketing. I've considered launching to my GoBundance community but I'm hesitant — missing features and my reputation is on the line.
  • Land Business (Paused)
  • Altura Land Services. Proven model — made real money with a partner before. Paused due to expensive mailers and the lowball-offer model conflicting with my win-win values. Unexplored idea: AI voice assistant to qualify inbound leads and filter for serious sellers.
  • Airbnb (Short-Term Rentals)
  • Several Utah properties on Hostaway. Overspent on furnishings, tough to cash flow — lots of management, not a lot of squeeze. Considering selling for cash, seller-financing, or optimizing. Hard lessons learned: a co-host who used the company card for personal expenses, then a 14-person VA team with zero accountability. Current co-host Karla is great — you'd work through her. She saves me time but the bottom line hasn't improved much.
  • Long-Term Rentals
  • Fourplex and additional properties. Straightforward — tenants, maintenance, documents.
  • Personal Admin
  • Always on my list but never gets done: Nomad Capitalist appointment, apartment research (Uruguay, Florida, Paraguay), Mexican passport, borrowing against silver/crypto, date planning, calendar, email, travel, expenses, relocation logistics.
  • Other Ideas (Not Active Yet)
  • Podcast Insight in Inmates (removing incarceration stigma), accessible Montessori education, and more. Factor these into your prioritization thinking.
  • Tools
  • Porch Light: Claude Code, GitHub, Firebase, App Store Connect, Google Play Console General: Google Workspace, WhatsApp, OpenClaw (AI assistant), Calendly STR: Hostaway, Airbnb, VRBO, Booking.com, PriceLabs, Turno, Ring, August Smart Locks, Google Home, Amazon Land: CRM, Launch Control, My Postcard Media, Traveling Mailbox, DocuSign, MLS, Land Portal Not exhaustive — request access as you need it.

description:
Technical Support Engineer II - eSign - Fluent French

 

Help French-speaking customers across Europe and beyond keep their most important agreements moving with Docusign eSignature.

 

Why this role matters

Every day, organizations rely on Docusign so their deals, HR offers, and critical agreements can be signed quickly and securely. As a French-speaking Technical Support Engineer based in Egypt, you are both a strategic customer advisor and the internal voice of our French-speaking customers. You’ll work with a truly global team and will use your fluent French and strong English daily to take ownership of complex technical cases, collaborate across teams, and help customers get maximum value from Docusign eSignature. 
 

What you'll do

  • Provide outstanding technical support for Docusign eSignature (eSign) and associated services.
  • Handle inbound support requests and troubleshoot issues such as sending/signing envelopes, digital signatures, templates, bulk sending, and use of the eSignature web app.
  • Use your broad product and technical knowledge to drive adoption and help customers get more from Docusign.
  • Work hands-on with tools such as Salesforce, Jira, Docusign logging systems, SharePoint, internal admin consoles, browser developer tools, SQL and text analysis tools.
  • Investigate and identify bugs, product gaps and high‑impact improvements to enhance the overall customer experience.
  • Promote Docusign products and services through consultative conversations based on customer needs.
  • Handle escalated cases from internal teams to resolve complex issues.
  • Support internal peers with product knowledge, troubleshooting approaches and best‑practice engagement paths.
  • Meet and exceed Docusign Customer Support service level goals for your eSign areas of expertise.
     

You’ll report to a Manager, Technical Support and work closely with Support, Engineering, Product, Customer Success and Sales.

 

How you’ll work

Job designation: Remote (Egypt)
You’ll work from a designated remote location in Egypt, collaborating virtually with teammates and customers across time zones. Roles at Docusign are designated as In Office, Hybrid or Remote based on the position; designations may change over time in line with business needs and local law.
requirements:
What you bring

 

Your core strengths: 

  • Fluent French with strong English (written and spoken) for daily collaboration across global teams.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or equivalent hands‑on experience in a technical support environment. 
  • 2+ years troubleshooting SaaS products in a Technical Support (or similar) role, preferably in a customer-facing capacity.
  • Comfortable working with web-based environments (HTTP, JSON, IIS, HTML, CSS) and at least some of: XML, C#, Python, PHP, JavaScript, jQuery.
  • Confident using log and network analysis tools (e.g. Wireshark, Fiddler, Charles Proxy, browser developer tools) to get to the root cause.
  • Exposure to Salesforce administration, Microsoft, Dynamics and/or SharePoint administration. 

 

Nice to Have: 

  • Deeper technical skills such as regular expressions, web service APIs, SQL / SOQL, Dynatrace or other monitoring tools, and authentication technologies like SAML and OAuth.
  • Industry experience in sectors where Docusign provides solutions.
  • Experience supporting Docusign eSignature or similar eSignature / agreement platforms, and familiarity with Docusign user models.
  • Strong communication skills with customers, peers and occasional executive stakeholders, especially in high‑pressure situations.
  • Certifications or experience such as Salesforce Admin, Microsoft System Administration, or Windows PowerShell scripting

description:

Main goals and responsibilities:

  • Be a proactive team worker;
  • Collaborate with the team to implement new features to support the growing data needs;
  • Build, maintain, and deploy DS and ML pipelines, including data retrieval, preprocessing, feature engineering, training, and inferencing the models, analyzing and visualizing the results;
  • Build, maintain and deploy LLM-powered systems and agent pipelines, including prompt engineering, tool and API integration, memory and context management, inference orchestration, and evaluation of model outputs;
  • Share knowledge with other teams on various data science or project-related topics;
  • Collaborate with the team to decide on which tools and strategies to use within specific scenarios.

 

What We Offer:

  • Long-term career stability with a competitive salary paid in USD.
  • Conditions for steady career development.
  • Development supported by dedicated mentors and a variety of programs focused on expertise and innovation.

  • A well-equipped and cozy office supports comfort and productivity across all project stages.

requirements:
Required:

  • English level upper intermediate+;

  • The contractor will be responsible for delivering a structured programme of quality investigations across the Source Based Project Management list throughout the eight-month engagement. Key outputs and expectations are defined as follows.
  • The contractor will conduct regular and systematic reviews of data held within RSP Resource Planner, Source Quick Suite, and the Planner Insight Reporting System, identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies across project records. All findings must be clearly documented, categorised by severity, and communicated back to the relevant stakeholder teams in a timely manner.
  • Where dashboard support is required, the contractor will contribute to the build and ongoing maintenance of reporting dashboards within Quick Suite, ensuring outputs are accurate, accessible, and aligned to the needs of the PMO, labour planning team, and OPT.
  • The contractor will maintain a live error log throughout the engagement, tracking identified issues, resolution status, and any recurring patterns that may indicate systemic data quality concerns. Regular progress updates will be provided to key partners across PMO, internal labour planning, OPT, and tech teams.
  • By the end of the engagement, the contractor will produce a consolidated quality findings report summarising key issues identified, resolutions applied, and recommendations for ongoing data governance.
requirements:
Required qualifications/certifications:

  • The ideal candidate will hold a bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Information Systems, Business Administration, Project Management, or a related field. A Project Management Professional (PMP) certification is strongly preferred and will be weighted heavily during selection, as it is the most recognized project management credential within multinational and PMO-aligned environments in Egypt. PRINCE2 Foundation or Practitioner will also be considered as an alternative, though PMP is the preferred standard for this engagement.
  • Candidates must demonstrate proven experience working with reporting and planning systems, with practical familiarity operating within resource planning tools and data visualization platforms. Experience building and maintaining dashboards within suite-based project management or reporting tools is essential. A certification in data analytics or business intelligence, such as Microsoft Power BI, Tableau Desktop Specialist, Google Data Analytics Certificate, or IBM Data Analyst Professional Certificate, is required. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel to an advanced level is also expected, including the use of pivot tables, lookups, and data validation techniques.
  • Strong capability in data analysis, error identification, root cause analysis, and structured reporting is required. Candidates with exposure to PMO frameworks or workforce planning environments will be prioritised. Familiarity with quality assurance methodologies such as Six Sigma Yellow or Green Belt is desirable but not mandatory.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English are required. 
  • The candidate must be based in Egypt and available to commit to the full 1-year contract duration.

 

Software / programs / tools: RSP / EXCEL / WORD

 

Required years of Experience: 3 years

 

Technical Assessment Needed: Yes

description:
About Us:  
At Guillam, we’re more than just a coffee house. We are a dynamic team of innovators, 
strategists, and creatives. Our team is driven by an unwavering passion for our 
products, people, and our customers. Every single day, we are committed to delivering 
exceptional experiences by seamlessly blending quality, creativity, and innovation in all. 


About the Role: 
We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly skilled Procurement Analyst to join our 
team. The ideal candidate will play a key role in monitoring stock levels, optimising 
purchasing decisions, and ensuring smooth inventory management across the 
business.  
This role requires a detail‑oriented individual with strong numerical and oraganisational 
skills, adept at leveraging data to uncover trends and provide actionable insights that 
drive smarter decision‑making. 


In this role, you will be able to: 
• Contribute to a company committed to redefining the coffee experience through 
operational excellence. 
• Thrive in a collaborative, fast-paced environment that values curiosity, 
ownership, and continuous improvement. 
• Grow your skills through hands-on learning, data-focused tasks, and exposure to 
real operational challenges. 
• Be part of a supportive team that encourages innovation, problem-solving, and 
personal development. 
• Engage in meaningful work that has a direct impact on business performance 
and efficiency. 

 

Responsibilities: 
• Monitor stock levels and lead times to ensure accurate inventory availability and 
timely purchasing. 
• Analyse what is being sold and usage patterns to optimise future orders and 
minimise overstock or shortages. 
• Track and report wastage levels, identifying root causes, discrepancies, and 
potential shrinkage, and recommend improvements. 
• Maintain strong relationships with suppliers, including evaluating which suppliers 
to use, tracking deliveries, and staying updated with new products and pricing. 
• Ensure proper storage, quality, and rotation of incoming stock, maintaining 
accurate documentation. 
• Conduct regular stock level checks and track equipment across locations to 
support operational efficiency and accurate inventory management. 
• Analyse available trading and procurement data to identify purchase patterns, 
lead times, cost implications, and credit terms.
